name: Mary Ann McCall
abstract: Mary Ann McCall (May 4, 1919 – December 14, 1994) was an American pop and jazz singer. Aside from solo work, she sang for Charlie Barnet, Tommy Dorsey, Stan Kenton, Artie Shaw, Teddy Powell, and Woody Herman.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz
Midnight Performance: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz
Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: she was with me, oh, I think around '40-'41... she made a lot of Bluebird records with me

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Igoe
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Ventura’s group with Mary Ann McCall singing. And so we played down south. We went on a 68 night tour with no breaks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003
